jokes.text: I was hoping we'd miss the 3:00 holdup.
jokes.setup: Homer threatens to shoot Apu over chip prices
jokes.punchline: Apu casually mentions there's a scheduled 3:00 holdup, treating robbery as routine
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery treating violent crime as mundane scheduling. Perfect economy and dark wit that's quintessentially Simpsons.

jokes.text: It's like a corn dog without the dog.
jokes.setup: Homer tries corn at a farm stand
jokes.punchline: He describes corn on the cob in the most backwards way possible
jokes.explanation: Homer's backwards logic perfectly captures his food-obsessed perspective. Simple but effective character comedy.

jokes.text: Put whatever you want in this burlap sack. / You may not. That's my baby carrier.
jokes.setup: Farmer offers a burlap sack for produce
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's actually being used as a baby carrier
jokes.explanation: Quick misdirection gag about rural poverty/practicality. Functional but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: This portobello mushroom eats like a steak. A rubbery, fungus-like steak.
jokes.setup: Homer tries to be positive about vegetarian food
jokes.punchline: His attempt at optimism immediately undercuts itself with honest disgust
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - trying to be supportive but unable to hide his revulsion. Great structure with immediate self-contradiction.

jokes.text: Marge, I'm giving birth to a food baby.
jokes.setup: Homer is having digestive issues from vegetarian food
jokes.punchline: Describes his stomach pain as 'giving birth to a food baby'
jokes.explanation: Common 'food baby' expression but fits Homer's crude humor. Serviceable but not particularly original.

jokes.text: I feel like something crawled inside me and took a crap. / Bart, don't use that word... / Crap.
jokes.setup: Bart uses crude language about his stomach pain, Marge objects
jokes.punchline: Marge objects to 'crap' while ignoring the more graphic imagery
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Marge focuses on the mild profanity while ignoring the truly disgusting metaphor. Perfect character logic and economy.

jokes.text: It's 'cause your bodies are so used to processed food, it's a shock when you eat vegetables full of vitamins, minerals and trace amounts of bug feces.
jokes.setup: Lisa explains why they're sick from healthy food
jokes.punchline: Lists healthy nutrients then casually adds 'bug feces'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Lisa's matter-of-fact delivery of gross truth. Structure works well building up healthy elements then undercutting.

jokes.text: It's staying down. It's not staying down. Wait. Yup, it's staying down. Oh, no, it's not. Yes, it is. No, it's not. Yup. No. Yup. No. Yup. No. Yup, no, yup, no, yup, no.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to keep food down
jokes.punchline: Increasingly frantic back-and-forth about his nausea status
jokes.explanation: Extended bit that goes on too long. The escalation works initially but becomes repetitive rather than building.

jokes.text: Do you have what it takes to be a singing sensation? A dynamite voice? Ruthless, pushy parents? A void in your self-esteem that can only be filled by applause?
jokes.setup: Krusty advertising a children's singing contest
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly dark psychological requirements for child stardom
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of child entertainment industry that builds from innocent to deeply cynical. Great escalation and character voice for Krusty.

jokes.text: Oh, God, I know that void.
jokes.setup: Krusty mentions void in self-esteem filled by applause
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately identifies with this psychological damage
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lisa's self-awareness about her need for validation. Dark and economical with great self-recognition.

jokes.text: Not affiliated with American Idol. We've never even heard of American Idol.
jokes.setup: Contest clearly mimics American Idol format
jokes.punchline: Obvious over-denial of any connection
jokes.explanation: Standard parody disclaimer joke. Functions well but fairly predictable meta-humor about obvious copying.

jokes.text: So enter today, especially if you're a funny-looking kid who doesn't know he sucks.
jokes.setup: Krusty encouraging kids to enter contest
jokes.punchline: Specifically targets delusional ugly children for exploitation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about reality TV exploitation. Perfectly captures Krusty's cynical opportunism while satirizing talent shows.

jokes.text: I think I puked up all of my meanness.
jokes.setup: Bart being unusually nice to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Attributes personality change to literally vomiting out his bad traits
jokes.explanation: Creative and character-specific explanation for Bart's rare kindness. Great absurdist logic with perfect economy.

jokes.text: Whenever I enter a contest, I tell myself I'm going to win because sometimes, if you believe hard enough, God cuts you a break. After all, He works for us. Our prayers pay His salary.
jokes.setup: Marge giving encouraging advice about believing in yourself
jokes.punchline: Escalates to treating God as an employee paid by prayer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from innocent faith to treating deity as service provider. Subversive theology with perfect build-up.

jokes.text: But seriously, cruelty to animals is no laughing matter. You can make a difference. Spay and neuter your pets. And remember, save the violence for cartoons.
jokes.setup: PSA about animal cruelty after violent cartoon
jokes.punchline: 'Save the violence for cartoons' - said during a cartoon
jokes.explanation: Self-aware meta-joke about cartoon violence. Good irony but somewhat obvious for Simpsons standards.

jokes.text: After this, I got to record 27 seasons of DVD commentaries, and I remember nothing.
jokes.setup: Krusty explaining why he wants to rush through auditions
jokes.punchline: References the real burden of DVD commentary recording
jokes.explanation: Smart meta-humor about the DVD commentary industry. Relatable for anyone who's heard rambling, uninformed commentary tracks.

jokes.text: I was going to be your celebrity judge, but then I realized I'd have to pay attention.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he's using an applause meter instead of judging
jokes.punchline: Admits he doesn't want the responsibility of paying attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect Krusty laziness and honesty about not caring. Great character integration with economic delivery.

jokes.text: I can't believe I used to date him.
jokes.setup: Bad singer performs terribly
jokes.punchline: Selma reveals she dated this loser
jokes.explanation: Good character-consistent joke about Selma's terrible taste in men. Works as both standalone and callback.

jokes.text: When she sings a 'C,' it's a 'C.' Unlike a certain glee club I've wasted my life on.
jokes.setup: Praising Clarissa's musical ability
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive dig at his regular glee club
jokes.explanation: Nice bit of character depth showing Krusty's bitterness about his regular commitments. Good structure and integration.

jokes.text: Which one are you, the man or the woman? / Questioning the kid's sexuality--well done.
jokes.setup: Homer sings 'When a man loves a woman' to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa questions the gender roles, Homer misinterprets as sexuality questioning
jokes.explanation: Homer's misunderstanding creates awkward humor. Character-appropriate but treads into uncomfortable territory without enough payoff.

jokes.text: Much of the tune is plagiarized.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks how he wrote a song so quickly
jokes.punchline: Homer casually admits to extensive plagiarism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty about his shortcuts. Great deadpan delivery and character consistency.

jokes.text: Except for Flanders.
jokes.setup: Song about how nobody in Springfield sucks
jokes.punchline: Lisa adds exception for Flanders
jokes.explanation: Standard Flanders-bashing callback. Gets the job done but very predictable for long-time viewers.

jokes.text: I wish I could say the same about my stockbroker.
jokes.setup: Says the audience is always right
jokes.punchline: Contrasts with his presumably wrong stockbroker
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity complaint humor. Fits Krusty's character but fairly generic rich person problems joke.

jokes.text: That's a quality joke.
jokes.setup: Krusty makes a mediocre joke about his stockbroker
jokes.punchline: He immediately self-evaluates it as 'quality'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about comedy self-evaluation. Krusty's delusional confidence about his mediocre material is perfect character work.

jokes.text: I wrote it about Shelbyville, then changed the names.
jokes.setup: Song praised Springfield, but Homer said he liked working with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Reveals he doesn't actually like Springfield, just copied a Shelbyville song
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic and laziness. Undercuts the emotional moment with typical corner-cutting, very character appropriate.

jokes.text: Well, Moe's getting a big screen TV in February, just in time for the Pro Bowl. Maybe this time my beloved blue shirts will win.
jokes.setup: Homer ominously says he won't be around much longer
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just because of TV sports scheduling, calls teams 'blue shirts'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from dramatic death hint to mundane sports priorities. 'Blue shirts' is perfect Homer sports ignorance. Great structure and character work.

jokes.text: He's cute, unthreatening, and his smile brought a puppy back to life.
jokes.setup: Describing why Cameron is popular with girls
jokes.punchline: Escalates from normal traits to supernatural puppy-reviving powers
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from realistic to absurd. The puppy detail is memorably weird and fits the boy band parody.

jokes.text: The song I wrote you is so schmaltzy, it'll make 'Moon River' sound like a farting orangutan.
jokes.setup: Homer promises Lisa a very sentimental song
jokes.punchline: Compares schmaltz levels using 'farting orangutan' as contrast to 'Moon River'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd comparison with great imagery. 'Farting orangutan' is memorably random while still serving the setup about schmaltz levels.

jokes.text: Disclaimer: All ballots were lost and vote totals made up.
jokes.setup: Announcing elimination based on audience votes
jokes.punchline: Casually admits all votes are fabricated
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of reality TV corruption delivered with perfect deadpan honesty. Great economy and cynical edge.

jokes.text: Fall into the flavor hole!
jokes.setup: Contestant falls through Buzz Cola trapdoor
jokes.punchline: Corporate slogan for what's essentially a child safety hazard
jokes.explanation: Good corporate speak parody turning elimination into branded experience. Absurdist but somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: My little girl likes Red Vines! / You little...!
jokes.setup: Manager brings wrong candy (Twizzlers vs Red Vines)
jokes.punchline: Homer escalates to violence over candy brand preference
jokes.explanation: Good character escalation showing Homer's stage parent transformation. The candy brand specificity adds realism to the absurd violence.

jokes.text: There's a hundred little punks graduating from Syracuse this year that would beg to kiss my ass.
jokes.setup: Homer threatening the manager during fight
jokes.punchline: Specific reference to Syracuse graduates as replacement threats
jokes.explanation: Good specificity with Syracuse detail. Shows Homer adopting entertainment industry attitude and language.

jokes.text: For once, couldn't you just turn into a good father? / Living through Lisa is the healthiest thing that ever happened to me.
jokes.setup: Bart asking Homer to be a better father
jokes.punchline: Homer claims living vicariously through Lisa is his healthiest behavior
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Homer's terrible parenting with perfect character logic. His twisted self-justification rings true.

jokes.text: Yeah. For once, couldn't you just turn into a good father? Would that be so hard?
jokes.setup: Marge criticizing Homer's behavior
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt assessment of Homer's parenting
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Bart cutting through Marge's diplomacy with brutal honesty. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: You love sausage, but you hate to see it getting made. / I don't love sausage. / Then would you like to see it getting made?
jokes.setup: Homer uses sausage metaphor about show business
jokes.punchline: Lisa's literal response derails the metaphor, Homer offers to show her actual sausage making
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection as Lisa's literal interpretation completely undermines Homer's attempt at wisdom. Great structure and character dynamics.

jokes.text: You were obnoxious at a level not even permitted in show business.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining why she fired Homer
jokes.punchline: Claims he exceeded even show business standards for obnoxiousness
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about show business tolerance levels. Lisa's articulate takedown is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Soon the whole world will be lining up to suck my nose.
jokes.setup: Homer boasting about his management success
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'kiss my ass' with 'brown nose' into 'suck my nose'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism creating absurd imagery. Character-specific verbal confusion that's both stupid and memorable.

jokes.text: So, are you a colonel or a captain? / Neither. I'm both.
jokes.setup: Homer calls himself Colonel Cool and captain
jokes.punchline: His logic-defying explanation that he's neither but both
jokes.explanation: Great Homer non-logic delivered with complete confidence. Perfect economy and absurdist character consistency.

jokes.text: Oh, God, they're delicious. Oh, I'm so happy. Oh, God, they're... They're gone.
jokes.setup: Homer tastes Lisa's apology cookies
jokes.punchline: Rapid emotional journey from joy to despair as he immediately devours them all
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer food obsession with good emotional arc. Predictable but well-executed character moment.

jokes.text: Hey, you're supposed to clean this out every night. / What do you mean, talk to your shop steward?
jokes.setup: Contestants trapped in trapdoor basement
jokes.punchline: Krusty dealing with union worker refusing to clean contestant pit
jokes.explanation: Good workplace humor about union bureaucracy interfering with child safety. Realistic absurdist detail.

jokes.text: Hey, you're supposed to clean this out every night. What do you mean, talk to your shop steward?
jokes.setup: Contestants trapped in elimination pit
jokes.punchline: Union issues preventing basic maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good workplace satire - bureaucracy preventing obvious solutions. Solid concept and execution.

jokes.text: Even the Applause-O-Meter is crying.
jokes.setup: Lisa sings emotional song about missing her father
jokes.punchline: The mechanical device somehow shows emotion
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist touch giving emotion to mechanical device. Simple but effective visual gag concept.

jokes.text: Even the Applause-O-Meter is crying. Oh, God, that's battery acid.
jokes.setup: Emotional reaction to Lisa's song
jokes.punchline: Machine malfunction mistaken for emotion
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - sets up touching moment then reveals mechanical reality. Good structure and timing.

jokes.text: Oh, God, that's battery acid.
jokes.setup: Applause-O-Meter appears to be crying
jokes.punchline: Reveals the 'tears' are actually dangerous battery acid
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection undermining the sweet moment with dangerous reality. Perfect economy and dark Simpsons humor.

jokes.text: Don't trust people in the music business.
jokes.setup: Homer about to sabotage Cameron
jokes.punchline: Gives advice that applies to himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - Homer proves his own point. Good economy and self-aware character moment.

jokes.text: Don't trust people in the music business.
jokes.setup: Homer about to sabotage Cameron's performance
jokes.punchline: Gives advice about not trusting music business while being untrustworthy
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Homer demonstrating the very untrustworthiness he warns about. Self-aware character moment.

jokes.text: I'd do anything for you, honey. Especially if it's easy.
jokes.setup: Homer shows his love by sabotaging Cameron
jokes.punchline: Qualifies his devotion with honest admission about preferring easy tasks
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character distillation - love combined with laziness. Excellent structure undercutting sentiment with honesty.

jokes.text: I'll always be there. Not even death will stop me. Now you're getting creepy. How 'bout I love you, honey. Good.
jokes.setup: Homer making romantic promises
jokes.punchline: Lisa calls out the inappropriate intensity, Homer adjusts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and correction. Lisa's directness grounds Homer's over-the-top sentiment perfectly.

jokes.text: Now you're getting creepy. / How 'bout I love you, honey. / Good.
jokes.setup: Homer makes dramatic promise about love beyond death
jokes.punchline: Lisa calls it creepy, Homer adjusts to normal parental affection
jokes.explanation: Nice father-daughter dynamic with Lisa keeping Homer grounded. Good character interaction showing healthy boundaries.
